DPNR Outplant Analyses

This report analyzes DPNR coral outplant monitoring data from TagLab match files. The workflow is designed for repeated use: add new plot/month files to the config, render the report, and the tables and figures rebuild automatically.

The current test case is Plot E, with TagLab subsections tracked separately. Section 1-1 currently runs from May through November, and section 1-0 currently runs from May through August. The analysis tracks outplant survivorship, interval survival, coral cover, species prevalence, species cover composition, temperature, and plot-level depth metadata.

The two recommended datasets to open first are:

  • data_processed/outplant_master_tracking_dataset.csv: one row per tracked Genet per month, including presence, area, cover contribution, and baseline-survival status.
  • outputs/Tables/outplant_master_summary_dataset.csv: one long-format summary table containing cumulative survivorship, interval survivorship, coral cover, species prevalence, and species cover-share metrics.

Each plot tab is organized with the same outline so new data can be added without redesigning the report: overview, data status, depth metadata, QA/QC, cumulative survivorship, interval survivorship, coral cover, species prevalence, species cover composition, and downloads.

  1. Export TagLab match files for each plot section/month pair using the same batch export process so Genet IDs stay consistent across months.
  2. Place the exported CSV files in data_raw/taglab/<Plot_Folder>/.
  3. Add one row per month-to-month match file in config/outplant_interval_files.csv.
  4. Confirm each config row has the correct plot name, plot section, plot folder, filename, start month, end month, file type, plot dimensions, and TagLab area units.
  5. Confirm data_raw/metadata/Coordinates_Depths.csv has marker depths for each plot if depth summaries will be used.
  6. Render this Quarto report.
  7. Check the file audit and QA/QC tables first.
  8. Confirm the shared-month continuity table has zero missing Genets, zero unexpected new Genets, and zero species mismatches.
  9. Use cumulative survivorship as the main survival result.
  10. Use interval survivorship as a diagnostic for each month-to-month transition.
  11. Open the two master datasets when exporting or sharing results.

This setup chunk loads the packages needed for table formatting and data manipulation, then sources the outplant interval workflow. Sourcing the script here means the report always rebuilds from the latest raw/config files when rendered.

Code 
library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)

# Find the project folder from the report location. This lets collaborators move
# the full project folder anywhere on their computer and still render the report.
report_path <- knitr::current_input(dir = TRUE)
report_dir <- if (!is.null(report_path)) {
  dirname(normalizePath(report_path, mustWork = TRUE))
} else {
  getwd()
}

project_root <- report_dir
while (!file.exists(file.path(project_root, "config", "outplant_interval_files.csv"))) {
  parent_dir <- dirname(project_root)
  if (identical(parent_dir, project_root)) {
    stop(
      "Could not find config/outplant_interval_files.csv. ",
      "Render this report from inside the Coral_survivorship_project folder.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }
  project_root <- parent_dir
}

knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = project_root)
setwd(project_root)

source(file.path(project_root, "R", "03_outplant_interval_workflow.R"))

Workflow

This report reads the outplant-only TagLab match files listed in config/outplant_interval_files.csv, standardizes them into common start/end area columns, collapses repeated blob rows to one tracked Genet per interval, and produces survivorship, cover, and species-composition outputs. Plot sections are analyzed separately so sections such as Plot E 1-0 and Plot E 1-1 do not get combined accidentally.

The workflow is designed so future users can add new plots or month pairs by editing the config rather than changing analysis code.

The two recommended outplant datasets to open first are:

  • data_processed/outplant_master_tracking_dataset.csv: one row per tracked Genet per month, including presence, area, cover contribution, and baseline-survival status.
  • outputs/Tables/outplant_master_summary_dataset.csv: one long-format summary table containing cumulative survivorship, interval survivorship, coral cover, species prevalence, and species cover-share metrics.

The workflow still writes detailed intermediate CSVs for QA/QC and reproducibility, but most users should only need the two files above unless they are debugging the analysis.

Survivorship is treated as apparent persistence through a TagLab interval: an outplant is counted as surviving when it has measurable area at the start and end of the interval.

Outplant-Only Plot E Workflow

This is the main test workflow for the outplant-only TagLab match files. It treats Area1 as the start-month area and Area2 as the end-month area, and calculates cover using the configured 16 by 30 m plot area. The same-batch TagLab exports use Genet as the across-month tracking ID. If a Genet has multiple blob rows in one interval, those rows are collapsed into one tracked outplant before survivorship is calculated. The area unit is read from config/outplant_interval_files.csv; the current test assumes TagLab areas are in square centimeters.

This chunk verifies that every outplant interval file listed in the config exists. This is the first place to check when adding new files for other plots or months.

Code 
outplant_file_audit %>%
  select(plot, plot_section, month_start, month_end, match_type, file, file_exists) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c("Plot", "Section", "Start month", "End month", "Match type", "File", "File exists")
  )
Plot Section Start month End month Match type File File exists
Plot E 1-1 May June 1-1 plotE1-1Matches_Coki_PlotE_20250507_1-1-Coki_PlotE_20250609_1-1.csv TRUE
Plot E 1-1 June July 1-1 plotE1-1Matches_Coki_PlotE_20250609_1-1-Coki_PlotE_20250711_1-1.csv TRUE
Plot E 1-1 July August 1-1 plotE1-1Matches_Coki_PlotE_20250711_1-1-Coki_PlotE_20250804_1-1.csv TRUE
Plot E 1-1 August September 1-1 plotE1-1Matches_Coki_PlotE_20250804_1-1-Coki_PlotE_20250908_1-1.csv TRUE
Plot E 1-1 September October 1-1 plotE1-1Matches_Coki_PlotE_20250908_1-1-Coki_PlotE_20251014_1-1.csv TRUE
Plot E 1-1 October November 1-1 plotE1-1Matches_Coki_PlotE_20251014_1-1-Coki_PlotE_20251106_1-1.csv TRUE
Plot E 1-0 May June 1-0 PlotC_1-0_Coki_PlotE_20250507_1-0-Coki_PlotE_20250609_1-0.csv TRUE
Plot E 1-0 June July 1-0 PlotC_1-0_Coki_PlotE_20250609_1-0-Coki_PlotE_20250711_1-0.csv TRUE
Plot E 1-0 July August 1-0 PlotC_1-0_Coki_PlotE_20250711_1-0-Coki_PlotE_20250804_1-0.csv TRUE

This chunk checks whether the TagLab files have missing Genet IDs, missing species, negative areas, or unusual action/split-fuse labels. These checks catch export problems before the biological summaries are interpreted.

Code 
outplant_interval_qa %>%
  select(
    plot_section,
    month_start,
    month_end,
    n_rows,
    n_missing_genet,
    n_missing_species,
    n_negative_area_start,
    n_negative_area_end,
    actions,
    split_fuse_events
  ) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c(
      "Section", "Start month", "End month", "Rows", "Missing Genet", "Missing species",
      "Negative start area", "Negative end area", "Actions", "Split/fuse events"
    )
  )
Section Start month End month Rows Missing Genet Missing species Negative start area Negative end area Actions Split/fuse events
1-0 May June 272 0 0 0 0 born; dead; grow; n/s; same; shrink fuse; n/s; none; split
1-0 June July 218 0 0 0 0 born; dead; grow; n/s; same; shrink fuse; n/s; none
1-0 July August 177 0 0 0 0 dead; grow; n/s; same; shrink fuse; n/s; none
1-1 May June 76 0 0 0 0 dead; grow; same; shrink n/s; none
1-1 June July 70 0 0 0 0 dead; grow; n/s; same; shrink fuse; n/s; none
1-1 July August 65 0 0 0 0 born; dead; grow; n/s; same; shrink fuse; n/s; none
1-1 August September 62 0 0 0 0 grow; n/s; same; shrink fuse; n/s
1-1 September October 59 0 0 0 0 dead; grow; n/s; same; shrink fuse; n/s; none
1-1 October November 57 0 0 0 0 born; grow; n/s; same; shrink fuse; n/s; none

This chunk checks whether the same Genet number maps to different species, tags, or genotypes across files. If the table says no conflicts were detected, the same-batch export is behaving much better for individual-level survivorship.

Code 
if (nrow(outplant_unstable_genet_examples) == 0) {
  tibble(status = "No cross-file Genet identity conflicts detected.") %>%
    kable(col.names = "Status")
} else {
  outplant_unstable_genet_examples %>%
    slice_head(n = 20) %>%
    kable(
      col.names = c(
        "Plot", "Section", "Section label", "Source file", "Start month", "End month", "Genet",
        "Tag", "Genotype", "Species", "Action", "Start area", "End area"
      )
    )
}
Status
No cross-file Genet identity conflicts detected.

This chunk checks whether the same shared month matches between adjacent files after repeated Genet blob rows have been collapsed. A good export should have zero missing/new Genets and zero species mismatches for each shared month. Area mismatches can occur when TagLab split/fuse behavior changes how multiple blobs are grouped under the same Genet.

Code 
outplant_shared_month_continuity_qa %>%
  select(
    plot_section,
    shared_month,
    previous_end_n,
    next_start_n,
    in_both_snapshots,
    missing_from_next_start,
    new_in_next_start,
    species_mismatches,
    area_mismatches,
    max_abs_area_difference
  ) %>%
  mutate(max_abs_area_difference = round(max_abs_area_difference, 4)) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c(
      "Section", "Shared month", "Previous file end n", "Next file start n",
      "In both", "Missing from next start", "New in next start",
      "Species mismatches", "Area mismatches", "Max area difference"
    )
  )
Section Shared month Previous file end n Next file start n In both Missing from next start New in next start Species mismatches Area mismatches Max area difference
1-0 June 184 174 174 10 0 0 13 70.040
1-0 July 164 170 164 0 6 0 25 151.760
1-1 June 55 55 55 0 0 0 0 0.000
1-1 July 54 54 54 0 0 0 2 556.780
1-1 August 55 55 55 0 0 0 1 545.685
1-1 September 55 55 55 0 0 0 2 55.855
1-1 October 54 54 54 0 0 0 3 34.745

This chunk summarizes cumulative outplant survivorship from the baseline month. May is treated as 100%, and later months report how many original May Genet IDs are still present.

Code 
outplant_cumulative_survival_summary %>%
  select(
    plot_section_label,
    month,
    baseline_month,
    n_baseline,
    n_died_interval,
    cumulative_deaths_observed,
    n_surviving_from_baseline,
    percent_cumulative_survival,
    percent_cumulative_survival_low,
    percent_cumulative_survival_high
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    percent_cumulative_survival = round(percent_cumulative_survival, 1),
    percent_cumulative_survival_low = round(percent_cumulative_survival_low, 1),
    percent_cumulative_survival_high = round(percent_cumulative_survival_high, 1)
  ) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c(
      "Plot section", "Month", "Baseline month", "Baseline n", "Deaths this interval",
      "Cumulative deaths", "Surviving from baseline",
      "Cumulative survival (%)", "95% CI low", "95% CI high"
    )
  )
Plot section Month Baseline month Baseline n Deaths this interval Cumulative deaths Surviving from baseline Cumulative survival (%) 95% CI low 95% CI high
Plot E 1-0 May May 210 0 0 210 100.0 98.3 100.0
Plot E 1-0 June May 210 29 29 181 86.2 80.8 90.6
Plot E 1-0 July May 210 18 47 163 77.6 71.4 83.1
Plot E 1-0 August May 210 3 50 160 76.2 69.8 81.8
Plot E 1-1 May May 61 0 0 61 100.0 94.1 100.0
Plot E 1-1 June May 61 6 6 55 90.2 79.8 96.3
Plot E 1-1 July May 61 1 7 54 88.5 77.8 95.3
Plot E 1-1 August May 61 1 8 53 86.9 75.8 94.2
Plot E 1-1 September May 61 0 8 53 86.9 75.8 94.2
Plot E 1-1 October May 61 0 8 53 86.9 75.8 94.2
Plot E 1-1 November May 61 0 8 53 86.9 75.8 94.2

This chunk summarizes the Deep Nursery temperature logger data used in the cumulative survivorship figure. The logger is slightly deeper than the outplant site but close enough for this practice overlay.

Code 
outplant_daily_temperature_summary %>%
  summarise(
    logger_location = first(logger_location),
    start_date = min(date),
    end_date = max(date),
    n_days = n(),
    mean_temperature_c = mean(mean_temperature_c, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_temperature_c = min(min_temperature_c, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_temperature_c = max(max_temperature_c, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    mean_temperature_c = round(mean_temperature_c, 2),
    min_temperature_c = round(min_temperature_c, 2),
    max_temperature_c = round(max_temperature_c, 2)
  ) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c(
      "Logger", "Start date", "End date", "Days",
      "Mean temp (°C)", "Min temp (°C)", "Max temp (°C)"
    )
  )
Logger Start date End date Days Mean temp (°C) Min temp (°C) Max temp (°C)
Deep Nursery 2025-05-07 2025-11-06 184 29.11 27.67 30.37

This chunk plots cumulative outplant survivorship from the baseline month. The red line shows daily mean temperature on the secondary y-axis.

Code 
outplant_cumulative_survival_plot

This chunk reports interval survivorship between adjacent survey months. It is useful as a diagnostic because a later interval can be 100% even if cumulative survivorship has already declined.

Code 
outplant_survival_summary %>%
  select(
    plot_section_label,
    interval_label,
    n_start,
    n_survived,
    n_died,
    n_born,
    percent_survival,
    percent_survival_low,
    percent_survival_high
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    percent_survival = round(percent_survival, 1),
    percent_survival_low = round(percent_survival_low, 1),
    percent_survival_high = round(percent_survival_high, 1)
  ) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c(
      "Plot section", "Interval", "Start n", "Survived n", "Died n", "Born n",
      "Interval survival (%)", "95% CI low", "95% CI high"
    )
  )
Plot section Interval Start n Survived n Died n Born n Interval survival (%) 95% CI low 95% CI high
Plot E 1-0 May to June 210 181 29 3 86.2 80.8 90.6
Plot E 1-0 June to July 174 163 11 1 93.7 89.0 96.8
Plot E 1-0 July to August 170 167 3 0 98.2 94.9 99.6
Plot E 1-1 May to June 61 55 6 0 90.2 79.8 96.3
Plot E 1-1 June to July 55 54 1 0 98.2 90.3 100.0
Plot E 1-1 July to August 54 53 1 2 98.1 90.1 100.0
Plot E 1-1 August to September 55 55 0 0 100.0 93.5 100.0
Plot E 1-1 September to October 55 54 1 0 98.2 90.3 100.0
Plot E 1-1 October to November 54 54 0 2 100.0 93.4 100.0

This chunk plots interval survivorship between adjacent survey months. This is why October-to-November can be 100% even when cumulative survivorship is lower.

Code 
outplant_survival_plot

This chunk reports outplant coral cover by month. Cover is calculated as summed outplant area divided by the 480 m2 plot area.

Code 
outplant_cover_summary %>%
  select(plot_section_label, month, n_outplants_present, total_outplant_area_m2, plot_area_m2, percent_outplant_cover) %>%
  mutate(
    total_outplant_area_m2 = round(total_outplant_area_m2, 6),
    percent_outplant_cover = round(percent_outplant_cover, 5)
  ) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c(
      "Plot section", "Month", "Outplants present", "Total outplant area (m2)",
      "Plot area (m2)", "Outplant cover (%)"
    )
  )
Plot section Month Outplants present Total outplant area (m2) Plot area (m2) Outplant cover (%)
Plot E 1-0 May 210 0.219992 480 0.04583
Plot E 1-0 June 184 0.225128 480 0.04690
Plot E 1-0 July 164 0.249760 480 0.05203
Plot E 1-0 August 167 0.191067 480 0.03981
Plot E 1-1 May 61 0.062082 480 0.01293
Plot E 1-1 June 55 0.055176 480 0.01150
Plot E 1-1 July 54 0.112497 480 0.02344
Plot E 1-1 August 55 0.120594 480 0.02512
Plot E 1-1 September 55 0.078416 480 0.01634
Plot E 1-1 October 54 0.078395 480 0.01633
Plot E 1-1 November 56 0.075911 480 0.01581

This chunk plots outplant cover through time. Because outplants are small relative to a 480 m2 plot, the percent cover values are expected to be very small.

Code 
outplant_cover_plot

This chunk summarizes species abundance and prevalence among present outplant annotations in each monthly snapshot. n_outplants is the abundance count, and percent_prevalence is that species’ share of present outplants for that month.

Code 
outplant_species_prevalence_summary %>%
  select(
    plot_section_label,
    month,
    species,
    n_outplants,
    percent_prevalence,
    total_outplant_area_m2,
    percent_cover_within_outplants
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    percent_prevalence = round(percent_prevalence, 1),
    total_outplant_area_m2 = round(total_outplant_area_m2, 6),
    percent_cover_within_outplants = round(percent_cover_within_outplants, 1)
  ) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c(
      "Plot section", "Month", "Species", "Outplant abundance", "Prevalence (%)",
      "Outplant area (m2)", "Share of outplant cover (%)"
    )
  )
Plot section Month Species Outplant abundance Prevalence (%) Outplant area (m2) Share of outplant cover (%)

This chunk plots species prevalence through time using counts of present outplant annotations.

Code 
outplant_species_prevalence_plot

This chunk plots each species’ share of total outplant cover through time. It complements prevalence because a species can have fewer annotations but more cover.

Code 
outplant_species_cover_plot

Plot Depth Metadata

This chunk summarizes the corner-marker depth metadata for every plot listed in data_raw/metadata/Coordinates_Depths.csv. These values describe the plot-level depth context and should not be interpreted as exact depths for individual coral colonies.

Code 
outplant_plot_depth_summary %>%
  mutate(
    mean_depth_ft = round(mean_depth_ft, 1),
    min_depth_ft = round(min_depth_ft, 1),
    max_depth_ft = round(max_depth_ft, 1),
    depth_range_ft = round(depth_range_ft, 1),
    mean_depth_m = round(mean_depth_m, 2),
    min_depth_m = round(min_depth_m, 2),
    max_depth_m = round(max_depth_m, 2),
    depth_range_m = round(depth_range_m, 2),
    centroid_latitude = round(centroid_latitude, 6),
    centroid_longitude = round(centroid_longitude, 6)
  ) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c(
      "Plot", "Depth markers", "Mean depth (ft)", "Min depth (ft)",
      "Max depth (ft)", "Depth range (ft)", "Mean depth (m)",
      "Min depth (m)", "Max depth (m)", "Depth range (m)",
      "Centroid latitude", "Centroid longitude"
    )
  )
Plot Depth markers Mean depth (ft) Min depth (ft) Max depth (ft) Depth range (ft) Mean depth (m) Min depth (m) Max depth (m) Depth range (m) Centroid latitude Centroid longitude
Plot A 4 40.2 30 51 21 12.27 9.14 15.54 6.40 18.35083 -64.86417
Plot B 4 37.8 25 51 26 11.51 7.62 15.54 7.92 18.35078 -64.86444
Plot C 4 33.5 22 45 23 10.21 6.71 13.72 7.01 18.35072 -64.86471
Plot D 4 31.8 22 42 20 9.68 6.71 12.80 6.10 18.35064 -64.86495
Plot E 4 31.8 22 42 20 9.68 6.71 12.80 6.10 18.35057 -64.86519
Plot F 4 30.0 20 41 21 9.14 6.10 12.50 6.40 18.35046 -64.86546
Plot G 4 29.0 20 38 18 8.84 6.10 11.58 5.49 18.35037 -64.86575
Plot H 4 27.8 19 38 19 8.46 5.79 11.58 5.79 18.35028 -64.86601

This chunk joins the species prevalence results to the plot-level depth summary. It is a setup table for future species-depth analyses once multiple plots have outplant match files configured.

Code 
outplant_species_depth_summary %>%
  select(
    plot,
    plot_section,
    month,
    species,
    n_outplants,
    percent_prevalence,
    percent_cover_within_outplants,
    mean_depth_ft,
    min_depth_ft,
    max_depth_ft,
    depth_range_ft
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    percent_prevalence = round(percent_prevalence, 1),
    percent_cover_within_outplants = round(percent_cover_within_outplants, 1),
    mean_depth_ft = round(mean_depth_ft, 1),
    min_depth_ft = round(min_depth_ft, 1),
    max_depth_ft = round(max_depth_ft, 1),
    depth_range_ft = round(depth_range_ft, 1)
  ) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c(
      "Plot", "Section", "Month", "Species", "Outplant abundance",
      "Prevalence (%)", "Share of outplant cover (%)", "Mean depth (ft)",
      "Min depth (ft)", "Max depth (ft)", "Depth range (ft)"
    )
  )
Plot Section Month Species Outplant abundance Prevalence (%) Share of outplant cover (%) Mean depth (ft) Min depth (ft) Max depth (ft) Depth range (ft)
Plot E 1-0 May Acropora cervicornis 14 6.7 4.0 31.8 22 42 20
Plot E 1-0 May Acropora palmata 43 20.5 30.8 31.8 22 42 20

Overview

Plot A is included as a placeholder for future outplant match-file analyses.

Data Status

No Plot A outplant match files are currently configured. Add Plot A rows to config/outplant_interval_files.csv, then render this report to populate this tab through the same workflow.

Depth Metadata

This section will show Plot A corner-marker depth summaries and plot-level depth-gradient context once Plot A outplant match files are configured.

QA/QC

This section will summarize file checks, missing values, split/fuse labels, Genet consistency, and shared-month continuity once Plot A data are added.

Cumulative Survivorship

This section will show the percentage of baseline Plot A outplants still present through time.

Interval Survivorship

This section will show month-to-month Plot A survivorship between adjacent TagLab match files.

Coral Cover

This section will show Plot A outplant cover through time using the configured plot area.

Species Prevalence

This section will show Plot A outplant abundance and prevalence by species through time.

Species Cover Composition

This section will show each species’ share of total Plot A outplant cover through time.

Downloads

This section will point to Plot A processed datasets, summary tables, and figures after Plot A data are configured.

Overview

Plot E is currently the configured test plot for the outplant-only TagLab match workflow. Sections 1-0 and 1-1 are analyzed separately.

Data Status

The active Plot E files are:

Code 
outplant_config %>%
  filter(plot == "Plot E") %>%
  select(plot, plot_section, month_start, month_end, month_start_date, month_end_date, file, plot_length_m, plot_width_m, taglab_area_units) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c(
      "Plot", "Section", "Start month", "End month", "Start date", "End date", "File",
      "Plot length (m)", "Plot width (m)", "Area units"
    )
  )
Plot Section Start month End month Start date End date File Plot length (m) Plot width (m) Area units
Plot E 1-1 May June 2025-05-07 2025-06-09 plotE1-1Matches_Coki_PlotE_20250507_1-1-Coki_PlotE_20250609_1-1.csv 16 30 cm2
Plot E 1-1 June July 2025-06-09 2025-07-11 plotE1-1Matches_Coki_PlotE_20250609_1-1-Coki_PlotE_20250711_1-1.csv 16 30 cm2
Plot E 1-1 July August 2025-07-11 2025-08-04 plotE1-1Matches_Coki_PlotE_20250711_1-1-Coki_PlotE_20250804_1-1.csv 16 30 cm2
Plot E 1-1 August September 2025-08-04 2025-09-08 plotE1-1Matches_Coki_PlotE_20250804_1-1-Coki_PlotE_20250908_1-1.csv 16 30 cm2
Plot E 1-1 September October 2025-09-08 2025-10-14 plotE1-1Matches_Coki_PlotE_20250908_1-1-Coki_PlotE_20251014_1-1.csv 16 30 cm2
Plot E 1-1 October November 2025-10-14 2025-11-06 plotE1-1Matches_Coki_PlotE_20251014_1-1-Coki_PlotE_20251106_1-1.csv 16 30 cm2
Plot E 1-0 May June 2025-05-07 2025-06-09 PlotC_1-0_Coki_PlotE_20250507_1-0-Coki_PlotE_20250609_1-0.csv 16 30 cm2
Plot E 1-0 June July 2025-06-09 2025-07-11 PlotC_1-0_Coki_PlotE_20250609_1-0-Coki_PlotE_20250711_1-0.csv 16 30 cm2
Plot E 1-0 July August 2025-07-11 2025-08-04 PlotC_1-0_Coki_PlotE_20250711_1-0-Coki_PlotE_20250804_1-0.csv 16 30 cm2

Depth Metadata

The Plot E depth summary is based on the four corner markers in data_raw/metadata/Coordinates_Depths.csv. These depths describe the plot-level setting for the active outplant workflow.

Code 
outplant_plot_depth_summary %>%
  filter(plot == "Plot E") %>%
  mutate(
    mean_depth_ft = round(mean_depth_ft, 1),
    min_depth_ft = round(min_depth_ft, 1),
    max_depth_ft = round(max_depth_ft, 1),
    depth_range_ft = round(depth_range_ft, 1),
    mean_depth_m = round(mean_depth_m, 2),
    min_depth_m = round(min_depth_m, 2),
    max_depth_m = round(max_depth_m, 2),
    depth_range_m = round(depth_range_m, 2)
  ) %>%
  select(
    plot,
    n_depth_markers,
    mean_depth_ft,
    min_depth_ft,
    max_depth_ft,
    depth_range_ft,
    mean_depth_m,
    min_depth_m,
    max_depth_m,
    depth_range_m
  ) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c(
      "Plot", "Depth markers", "Mean depth (ft)", "Min depth (ft)",
      "Max depth (ft)", "Depth range (ft)", "Mean depth (m)",
      "Min depth (m)", "Max depth (m)", "Depth range (m)"
    )
  )
Plot Depth markers Mean depth (ft) Min depth (ft) Max depth (ft) Depth range (ft) Mean depth (m) Min depth (m) Max depth (m) Depth range (m)
Plot E 4 31.8 22 42 20 9.68 6.71 12.8 6.1

QA/QC

The full Plot E QA/QC tables are shown in the Main Work Flow tab. These include file checks, interval QA, Genet consistency, and shared-month continuity.

Cumulative Survivorship

The Plot E cumulative survivorship table and figure are shown in the Main Work Flow tab.

Interval Survivorship

The Plot E interval survivorship table and figure are shown in the Main Work Flow tab.

Coral Cover

The Plot E outplant cover table and figure are shown in the Main Work Flow tab.

Species Prevalence

The Plot E species prevalence table and figure are shown in the Main Work Flow tab.

Species Cover Composition

The Plot E species cover-composition figure is shown in the Main Work Flow tab.

Downloads

Use the master tracking dataset and master summary dataset listed in the Home tab for Plot E results.
